title: USD Layer-Stack Namespace Source Contract

included: wrapping a root document-model Layer plus already-opened recursive sublayer Layers as a NamespaceSource
building a recursive layer stack from subLayers metadata in already-opened Layers
detecting cycles across recursive subLayers while constructing the in-memory stack
reporting subLayerOffsets paired with subLayers without applying retiming
source capability declaration as layer_stack with partial composition
prim and property contributing-spec discovery across the stack
effective population specifier selection for stage-population query flags
strongest authored scalar/token/bool population field exposure
cross-layer authored listOp field composition for matching listOp field types
ordered child prim name composition across layer-stack specs
ordered property name composition across layer-stack specs
property field maps using strongest authored scalar fields and composed authored listOp fields
diagnostics for missing or duplicate supplied sublayers
deferred: reference, payload, inherits, specializes, and variant composition
attribute value resolution, time sample interpolation, and subLayerOffsets retiming

model.LayerStackNamespaceSource: A namespace-source provider backed by a root document-model Layer and its already-opened recursive sublayer Layers.
model.LayerStackEntry: One layer in the stack, with an identifier, strength index, and optional Retiming from the immediate authoring layer's subLayerOffsets field.
model.StrengthOrder: The root layer is strongest. Recursive sublayers are weaker than the layer that authored them, ordered depth-first at the authored subLayer site before later sibling sublayers.
model.ContributingSpecs: The strongest-to-weakest ordered specs at one path across stack entries. Queries may iterate them in reverse when applying ordered-child merge rules.
model.EffectiveSpecifier: Population-facing specifier summary: class if the strongest contributing defining specifier is class, def if any contributing def exists and no stronger class applies, otherwise over.
model.ComposedListOpField: A population-facing field value produced by combining same-typed authored listOp field opinions from contributing specs in strong-over-weak layer-stack order.
model.ComposedChildNames: Child prim names merged from weakest contributing spec to strongest, applying each spec's primOrder after that spec contributes primChildren.
model.ComposedPropertyNames: Union of contributing property names, sorted by path element ordering, then reordered by the strongest authored propertyOrder.

input.sublayers: already-opened document-model Layers keyed or ordered by authored subLayer asset identifiers for all recursive sublayers that may be reached from the root

output.layers: strong-to-weak layer entries with identifier, strengthIndex, offset, and scale; recursive sublayers appear depth-first immediately after the layer that authored them

output.value: strongest layer-stack field value or missing

output.exists: whether any layer-stack entry contributes a layer or prim spec at the path
output.childNames: composed child prim names before stage mask/active filtering
output.propertyNames: composed property names before schema fallback injection
output.contributingLayers: strong-to-weak identifiers that contributed a spec at this path

output.exists: whether any layer-stack entry contributes an attribute or relationship spec at the path
output.contributingLayers: strong-to-weak identifiers that contributed a property spec at this path

input.path: absolute prim path, variant-selection prim path, or /

output.opinions: strong-to-weak layer-stack scene-object opinions at this path with layer identifier, spec path, fields, child names, and property names

laws: the layer-stack source consumes document-model Layers and must not maintain a second independent durable spec store
subLayer asset identifiers are matched to already-opened layers at this boundary; this skill must not open files or call an asset resolver
the source traverses authored subLayers recursively only through layers already supplied at this boundary
recursive sublayer cycles must fail clearly, include the authored asset identifier that would close the cycle, and must not recurse unboundedly
root layer is stronger than every recursive sublayer
recursive sublayers are ordered depth-first at the authored subLayer site, before later sibling sublayers from the same authoring layer
subLayerOffsets are paired with subLayers for reporting, but are not applied to time samples, splines, clips, timecode-valued fields, or attribute values
a prim path exists if any layer-stack entry contributes a prim spec at that path; / exists as the root layer scene object
a property path exists if any layer-stack entry contributes an attribute or relationship spec at that path
effective specifier must preserve weaker def/class definitions through stronger over specs
for scalar, token, bool, enum/domain, and opaque payload field keys, field maps expose the strongest authored field value
for authored listOp field keys with matching listOp element types, field maps expose the listOp produced by combining contributing listOps in strong-over-weak order
dictionary merging, time-sample value resolution, spline value resolution, and non-listOp value composition remain deferred
childNames returned by this source are already composition-ordered; stage population must preserve this order for sources declaring child_ordering=composed
propertyNames returned by this source are already composition-ordered; stage population must preserve this order for sources declaring property_ordering=composed
child prim names are merged by iterating contributing specs from weakest to strongest, appending primChildren, uniquifying by first occurrence, then applying that spec's primOrder
property names are the union of contributing spec properties, sorted with usd-paths path element ordering, then reordered by the strongest authored propertyOrder
relocates authored on layer specs must remain inert field data in this first layer-stack provider
diagnostics from supplied layer construction are preserved and exposed through the source

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with rootLayer, optional sublayers map/list, and an ops array. The adapter must convert JSON layers into target-native document-model Layers before constructing the source.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results.
